ad_absurdum.overextension: If ‘so all may eat’ is treated as an unlimited claim on finite stores, contribution and stewardship disappear; the common table is consumed until nobody can eat.
ad_absurdum.counter_overextension: If contribution is made an absolute precondition for food, incapacity, scarcity, caregiving, injury, childhood, and temporary dependence become punishable by hunger.
ad_absurdum.boundary: Provide the survival floor first, then distinguish inability, refusal, fraud, and active harm through evidence, proportion, and review.

worst_practices: Require degrading proof of deservingness before meeting an immediate survival need.
Treat disability, exhaustion, caregiving, unemployment, or scarcity as deliberate refusal.
Let the person controlling supplies define contribution and harm without appeal.
Confuse equal human worth with identical allocation regardless of need or resource limits.
best_practices: Stabilize hunger or immediate danger before conducting eligibility or accountability review.
Separate need, capacity, contribution, refusal, and harmful conduct in the record.
Publish resource limits, allocation rules, exceptions, and an appeal path.
Use the least destructive response that protects the shared capacity to provide.

applications.Medical: Provide stabilizing care before billing, compliance, or character judgments; determine whether nonparticipation reflects incapacity, fear, access barriers, or refusal.
applications.Construction: Treat water, sanitation, rest, fall protection, and protective equipment as a floor while assigning work according to demonstrated capacity.
applications.Engineering: Reserve minimum safe operating resources before optimization and design graceful degradation so the least powerful user is not the first excluded.
applications.Agriculture: Protect seed, water, soil, feed, and subsistence reserves before market allocation; distinguish crop failure from neglect and extraction.
applications.Jurisprudence: Do not condition food, shelter, emergency medicine, or access to process on confession, status performance, or inability to pay.
applications.Transportation and distribution: Publish priority rules for scarce deliveries, preserve emergency stock and cold chain, and record why loads were rerouted or refused.
applications.Child craft: Meals, safety, affection, and basic learning materials remain unconditional; contribution is age-appropriate participation, not payment for care.
applications.Information systems: Maintain a minimum service level, accessible recovery path, and fair rate limits before premium optimization or anti-abuse escalation.
applications.Emergency response: Triage life threats first, then document conduct, capacity, and resource claims for later accountability.
applications.Hospitality and sanitation: Guarantee potable water, toilets, handwashing, waste removal, and basic food safety before aesthetic upgrades.
applications.Community governance: Maintain a visible survival floor and a transparent contribution ledger that includes care, maintenance, teaching, and risk-bearing work.

ad_absurdum.overextension: If ‘do nothing against your will’ means no obligation can ever survive reluctance, essential care, maintenance, sanitation, and teaching are abandoned whenever they become difficult.
ad_absurdum.counter_overextension: If every necessary task creates an enforceable duty on any available person, necessity becomes a universal excuse for forced labor.
ad_absurdum.boundary: Make necessary work visible, preserve refusal and exit, develop competence, distribute burdens, and escalate only when a specific preventable harm cannot otherwise be covered.

worst_practices: Rename coercion as teamwork, professionalism, family duty, or emergency whenever consent is inconvenient.
Hide essential labor until the same low-status person repeatedly absorbs it.
Treat trauma, poverty, dependence, or threat as irrelevant to whether a choice is voluntary.
Assign responsibility without tools, time, authority, training, or a safe way to decline.
best_practices: List the necessary tasks, capacities, risks, and unclaimed burdens before assigning people.
Use voluntary selection, rotation, mutual aid, training, and compensation before compulsion.
Preserve a meaningful refusal path and record the actual consequence of an uncovered task.
Separate immediate stabilization from redesign of the system producing repeated emergencies.

applications.Medical: Use informed choice, shared decision-making, team role clarity, and escalation paths while ensuring urgent symptoms are not ignored.
applications.Construction: Make task hazards and qualifications visible, allow stop-work authority, rotate undesirable work, and correct missing tools before blaming labor.
applications.Engineering: Define essential maintenance ownership, on-call limits, fallback capacity, and handoff requirements before failure.
applications.Agriculture: Plan labor, water, animal care, harvest, and repair duties around seasonal necessity while protecting rest, refusal, and contingency coverage.
applications.Jurisprudence: Use narrowly tailored civic duties with notice, competence support, exemptions, review, and proportional consequences.
applications.Transportation and distribution: Publish essential-route coverage, fatigue limits, relief crews, and refusal procedures for unsafe loads or conditions.
applications.Child craft: Teach necessary household and social skills through participation, modeling, choice within boundaries, and developmentally appropriate responsibility.
applications.Information systems: Define service ownership, incident rotation, maintenance windows, and user-impact priorities with sustainable staffing.
applications.Emergency response: Use predeclared roles, mutual-aid agreements, relief cycles, and lawful emergency authority with after-action review.
applications.Hospitality and sanitation: Make cleaning, food safety, laundry, and waste work visible, staffed, equipped, compensated, and rotatable.
applications.Community governance: Maintain a public task board showing what survival work exists, what is covered, what is unclaimed, and what support would make participation possible.

public_title: Article Three — Speech as demonstrated consent

ad_absurdum.overextension: If every hostile word authorizes identical treatment in return, one insult becomes a license for an escalating chain of retaliation.
ad_absurdum.counter_overextension: If no conduct may shape the response it receives, chronic intimidation and manipulation gain protection from proportionate boundaries.
ad_absurdum.boundary: Treat speech and conduct as evidence about interaction—not blanket consent. Clarify the mode, respond proportionately, preserve non-escalatory exits, and distinguish boundary-setting from revenge.

worst_practices: Infer consent to violence, humiliation, sex, punishment, or permanent exclusion from tone or prior conduct.
Mirror aggression automatically and call retaliation reciprocity.
Use politeness demands to silence accurate warnings, anger, grief, or dissent.
Judge wording without accounting for disability, language, culture, trauma, urgency, or power.
best_practices: Name the interaction mode: question, request, command, warning, accusation, joke, negotiation, or emergency direction.
Confirm meaning and desired response before escalating ambiguous speech.
Use the smallest proportionate boundary that stops harm and keeps repair possible.
Record threats, commands, consent, refusals, and handoffs in high-consequence settings.

applications.Medical: Use closed-loop communication, teach-back, escalation phrases, and explicit consent; treat distress speech as clinical information rather than permission to retaliate.
applications.Construction: Standardize stop-work calls, hazard warnings, hand signals, read-backs, and supervisor acknowledgment.
applications.Engineering: Label requirements, assumptions, objections, decisions, and unresolved risks; separate critique of a design from attack on a person.
applications.Agriculture: Use clear animal-handling, machinery, chemical, weather, and harvest signals across language and literacy differences.
applications.Jurisprudence: Distinguish protected speech, threat, instruction, consent, confession, and coercion with context and evidentiary standards.
applications.Transportation and distribution: Use standardized dispatch, load, route, exception, and emergency messages with read-back for consequential changes.
applications.Child craft: Teach children to name feelings, boundaries, requests, refusals, and repair while adults retain responsibility for proportional response.
applications.Information systems: Separate alerts, warnings, errors, approvals, and destructive confirmations; preserve logs and explicit user intent.
applications.Emergency response: Use plain command language, role identifiers, repeat-backs, and explicit transfer of command.
applications.Hospitality and sanitation: Standardize allergy, contamination, shortage, spill, and harassment reporting with no-retaliation acknowledgment.
applications.Community governance: Publish conversation rules that protect dissent, require definition of serious accusations, and distinguish debate from threat.

public_title: Article Four — Law as teachable duty

ad_absurdum.overextension: If every person must fully know every rule before acting, lawful participation becomes impossible and specialists monopolize ordinary life.
ad_absurdum.counter_overextension: If institutions have no duty to teach the rules they enforce, secret, shifting, or unintelligible law becomes a trap.
ad_absurdum.boundary: Teach the rules relevant to a person’s role and risk in language they can use; preserve notice, questions, examples, version history, and a defense when the system failed to make the duty knowable.

worst_practices: Punish first and explain the governing rule afterward.
Publish rules only in technical language, inaccessible formats, or scattered locations.
Change requirements silently while continuing enforcement.
Treat a signature, checkbox, or posted notice as proof of comprehension.
best_practices: Use plain language, examples, nonexamples, translated and accessible formats.
Require teach-back or demonstrated use for high-consequence duties.
Version rules, announce changes, and preserve the rule that applied at the time.
Create a question, correction, and appeal path before escalating punishment.

applications.Medical: Explain diagnosis, options, risks, discharge duties, medication use, and return precautions in usable language with teach-back.
applications.Construction: Teach site rules through orientation, demonstration, signage, toolbox talks, and supervisor verification.
applications.Engineering: Make standards, interfaces, assumptions, tolerances, change control, and safety constraints available at the point of work.
applications.Agriculture: Teach chemical, animal, machinery, water, labor, and food-safety rules in the languages and media workers actually use.
applications.Jurisprudence: Provide notice of charges, applicable rules, process, consequences, representation, and appeal in plain language.
applications.Transportation and distribution: Train and verify route, load, hours, dangerous-goods, customs, and exception procedures before assignment.
applications.Child craft: State boundaries and reasons in developmentally appropriate language, model them, and revisit them as capacity grows.
applications.Information systems: Put permissions, data use, security duties, destructive actions, and recovery steps where decisions occur.
applications.Emergency response: Use preincident training, quick-reference job aids, briefings, and just-in-time instruction under a common command vocabulary.
applications.Hospitality and sanitation: Teach food temperatures, allergens, cleaning chemistry, exposure response, and reporting through demonstration and visible job aids.
applications.Community governance: Publish rules, definitions, meeting procedure, decision authority, records, and remedies in one accessible place.

public_title: Article Five — Adulthood as demonstrated competence

ad_absurdum.overextension: If a single competence test defines adulthood everywhere, one institution can erase adult standing through cultural bias, disability exclusion, or irrelevant performance.
ad_absurdum.counter_overextension: If age or title alone establishes competence, people may wield consequential authority without the skills to avoid foreseeable harm.
ad_absurdum.boundary: Assess competence by field, task, context, and consequence using multiple demonstrations, support, accommodations, transparent criteria, and periodic reevaluation.

worst_practices: Use one test, one language, one body type, or one educational pathway as the universal adult standard.
Confuse credentials, confidence, seniority, wealth, or obedience with competence.
Make failure permanent or hide what successful demonstration requires.
Remove support in order to test independence and then label predictable harm incapacity.
best_practices: Define observable field-specific competencies and the harms they are meant to prevent.
Allow portfolios, simulation, supervised practice, oral, written, physical, and assistive demonstrations as appropriate.
Provide instruction, accommodations, retesting, and bounded scopes of practice.
Reevaluate competence when the task, environment, technology, health, or consequence changes.

applications.Medical: Use role-specific competencies, supervised practice, continuing assessment, and scope limits while incorporating patient-centred communication and contextual functioning.
applications.Construction: Verify task-specific skills for equipment, fall protection, excavation, electrical work, lifting, and emergency response.
applications.Engineering: Require traceable design judgment, review, testing, failure analysis, and domain-specific authorization.
applications.Agriculture: Demonstrate seasonal, machinery, animal, soil, water, chemical, preservation, and market competencies in context.
applications.Jurisprudence: Separate legal capacity, task competence, representation needs, and authority; use periodic, contextual evaluation rather than global status labels.
applications.Transportation and distribution: Verify vehicle, route, load, fatigue, documentation, maintenance, and emergency competencies by role.
applications.Child craft: Use developmental progression: model, practice, supervised demonstration, expanding autonomy, and revisable responsibility.
applications.Information systems: Use least privilege tied to demonstrated operational, security, privacy, and recovery competence.
applications.Emergency response: Credential roles before incidents, verify perishable skills, and assign authority only within demonstrated capability.
applications.Hospitality and sanitation: Recognize food safety, cleaning chemistry, infection control, de-escalation, maintenance, and guest-care competencies as skilled work.
applications.Community governance: Require demonstrated comprehension of duties, conflicts, records, budgets, consent, and consequences before granting public authority.

ad_absurdum.overextension: If authority is justified whenever force can impose an outcome, successful violence becomes self-legitimating tyranny.
ad_absurdum.counter_overextension: If nobody may hold bounded authority even during imminent danger, coordination collapses and the most forceful unofficial actor governs anyway.
ad_absurdum.boundary: Name who may decide, by what selection, for which scope, for how long, with what records, relief, oversight, liability, and transfer of authority.

worst_practices: Declare a permanent emergency or let temporary command expand into ordinary governance.
Select authority opaquely, by confidence, proximity to force, or personal loyalty.
Hide decisions, casualties, exceptions, and conflicts from later review.
Glorify decisiveness while treating doubt, humor, consultation, or relief as weakness.
best_practices: Predeclare scope, triggers, succession, limits, and termination conditions.
Separate command authority from investigation, review, and adjudication.
Log material decisions and preserve challenge, whistleblowing, and after-action review.
Rotate, relieve, support, and hold the authority-bearer accountable for foreseeable misuse.

applications.Medical: Clarify attending, nursing, specialist, patient, surrogate, ethics, and emergency decision authority with escalation and handoff.
applications.Construction: Name competent persons, stop-work authority, lift directors, safety leads, and incident command before hazardous work.
applications.Engineering: Assign design authority, independent review, change approval, safety sign-off, and rollback ownership.
applications.Agriculture: Define authority for animal emergencies, chemical incidents, fire, water allocation, harvest decisions, and equipment shutdown.
applications.Jurisprudence: Constrain coercive authority through jurisdiction, evidence, hearing, reasons, appeal, recusal, and liability.
applications.Transportation and distribution: Define dispatch, captain, driver, maintenance, loadmaster, customs, and emergency authority with explicit handoff.
applications.Child craft: Adults carry protective authority as a burden bounded by development, explanation, proportionality, and the child’s growing agency.
applications.Information systems: Use scoped administrative roles, dual control for destructive actions, immutable logs, incident command, and timely revocation.
applications.Emergency response: Apply common command, span of control, unified coordination, transfer briefings, resource tracking, and demobilization.
applications.Hospitality and sanitation: Name who can close service, discard food, isolate contamination, remove a dangerous person, or summon emergency help.
applications.Community governance: Select bounded decision-makers through visible process, record mandate and dissent, and require periodic return of authority.

ad_absurdum.overextension: If every word must be finally defined before conversation begins, discussion never starts and living language is frozen by gatekeepers.
ad_absurdum.counter_overextension: If consequential terms never need definition, power can change their meaning midstream and make agreement, consent, or accountability impossible.
ad_absurdum.boundary: Define the terms that control rights, duties, evidence, thresholds, and outcomes; include examples, exclusions, versioning, and a visible path for principled disagreement.

worst_practices: Use dictionary selection as an ambush after the other party has relied on a different meaning.
Let the most powerful participant define contested terms unilaterally.
Hide value judgments inside apparently neutral technical definitions.
Demand exhaustive definition of ordinary speech to delay or derail action.
best_practices: Identify which terms are load-bearing for the present decision.
State scope, examples, nonexamples, thresholds, and who may revise the definition.
Distinguish descriptive, technical, legal, moral, and local senses.
Record dissent and test whether different definitions would change the outcome.

applications.Medical: Define diagnosis, risk, benefit, success, capacity, emergency, standard care, and patient goals before consequential decisions.
applications.Construction: Define competent person, qualified person, acceptable condition, completion, defect, hazard, and stop-work threshold in contract and field use.
applications.Engineering: Use controlled vocabularies, units, interface contracts, requirement levels, assumptions, and acceptance criteria.
applications.Agriculture: Define organic, regenerative, humane, drought, pest threshold, yield, soil health, water right, and contamination for the local system.
applications.Jurisprudence: Define elements, burdens, standards, jurisdiction, harm, consent, reasonableness, and remedy before applying sanctions.
applications.Transportation and distribution: Define delivered, accepted, damaged, delayed, hazardous, perishable, secure, custody, and transfer timestamps.
applications.Child craft: Define family rules with concrete examples and revisit meanings as language and developmental capacity change.
applications.Information systems: Adopt explicit requirement words, schemas, threat definitions, severity levels, data ownership, availability targets, and done criteria.
applications.Emergency response: Use common terminology for command, resources, locations, priorities, triage, evacuation, and status.
applications.Hospitality and sanitation: Define clean, sanitized, allergen-free, safe temperature, ready, occupied, contaminated, and closed.
applications.Community governance: Maintain a public glossary for authority, adult, citizen, harm, consent, quorum, majority, conflict, and appeal, with revision history.

public_title: Article Eight — Rights with living limits

ad_absurdum.overextension: If ‘harm none’ means zero possible risk, movement, medicine, construction, agriculture, transport, speech, and ordinary life become impermissible.
ad_absurdum.counter_overextension: If consent alone makes any action right, coercion, misinformation, dependency, externalized harm, and irreversible effects disappear behind a checkbox.
ad_absurdum.boundary: Define relevant harms, affected parties, probabilities, reversibility, alternatives, ecological effects, comprehension, voluntariness, and withdrawal; protect diminished autonomy without erasing agency.

worst_practices: Use harm as an undefined accusation or require certainty before acknowledging foreseeable risk.
Treat a signature, click, silence, payment, dependency, or prior behavior as sufficient informed consent.
Invoke nature to excuse preventable suffering or ignore human-caused externalities.
Count only direct immediate harm to consenting parties while exporting costs to others or the future.
best_practices: Specify who may be harmed, how, with what likelihood, duration, reversibility, and evidence.
Separate information, comprehension, voluntariness, capacity, permission, and continuing assent.
Use least-harm alternatives, monitoring, stop conditions, remedy, and withdrawal.
Include indirect, cumulative, ecological, and distributional effects in the rights analysis.

applications.Medical: Use informed consent, capacity support, surrogate protection, risk-benefit review, alternatives, continuing assent, and withdrawal.
applications.Construction: Balance property, schedule, worker safety, public exposure, environmental effects, and stop-work rights through documented risk controls.
applications.Engineering: Build safety, privacy, security, accessibility, environmental, misuse, and rollback analysis into requirements and verification.
applications.Agriculture: Assess worker, animal, soil, water, biodiversity, consumer, neighbor, and future-season effects alongside owner consent and yield.
applications.Jurisprudence: Define protected interests, coercion, necessity, proportionality, informed waiver, standing, remedy, and the rights of affected nonparties.
applications.Transportation and distribution: Balance mobility and commerce against worker fatigue, public safety, emissions, access, dangerous goods, and community burden.
applications.Child craft: Use age-appropriate information, assent, protective limits, privacy, bodily autonomy, and periodic reevaluation as capacity grows.
applications.Information systems: Use specific, revocable, comprehensible consent plus data minimization, purpose limits, security, accessibility, and protection from dark patterns.
applications.Emergency response: Use necessity and proportionality, document rights restrictions, preserve humane treatment, and terminate extraordinary measures promptly.
applications.Hospitality and sanitation: Protect bodily safety, privacy, accessibility, food safety, worker rights, and environmental health while preserving reasonable guest choice.
applications.Community governance: Require public harm definitions, affected-party voice, minority protections, reversible pilots where possible, and remedies for imposed costs.
